skill.name: gmail-tools
skill.description: Read, search, and reply to Gmail messages using Gmail app passwords stored in 1Password.
skill.summary: Manage Gmail messages from an AI assistant with IMAP, SMTP, threading, and attachment support.
skill.author: ClementWalter

security_audit.summary: The static findings are mostly legitimate capability indicators, not confirmed malicious behavior. The skill accesses Gmail app passwords through the 1Password CLI, reads mail through IMAP, and sends replies through SMTP, so it needs clear user consent and a marketplace warning.

medium_findings.title: Gmail App Password Access Through 1Password CLI
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: the scripts intentionally call the 1Password CLI to retrieve Gmail usernames and app passwords. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it grants mailbox access if invoked by an untrusted assistant or workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly executes op item get and parses username and password fields. The documentation confirms that these are Gmail app password credentials.

medium_findings.title: Mailbox Content Read Over IMAP
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: the scripts connect to Gmail IMAP and fetch full message bodies. This matches the skill purpose, but it exposes private email content to the local assistant session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The IMAP calls target imap.gmail.com and fetch RFC822 messages. No third party destination was found, but mailbox data is still sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Email Sending and Attachment Transmission
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: the reply script can send SMTP replies and include local file attachments. The code has a dry-run option, but the normal path sends email to the original sender without an additional confirmation prompt.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SMTP_SSL call and send_message operation are direct evidence of email sending. Attachment handling reads local files when paths exist.

low_findings.title: External Command Injection Finding Is Limited
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for command injection: subprocess.run uses an argument list with the fixed op command and does not invoke a shell. The remaining risk is dependency on local 1Password authorization.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command arguments are passed as a list and shell=True is not used. The user-controlled item name is an argument to op, not shell syntax.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the reported weak-crypto locations do not implement cryptography. The relevant encoding in the reply script is MIME base64 attachment encoding, not password hashing or encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited script lines are argument parser setup or MIME transport encoding. No evidence of hashing, encryption, or custom cryptographic verification was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Are Benign
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: most SKILL.md external command matches are fenced examples showing how to use the scripts or text examples of tool parameters. They are not hidden execution directives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious matches appear inside normal documentation and code fences. I found no prompt injection, override text, or disguised system instruction.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Access Combined With Mailbox Network Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines local credential retrieval, Gmail IMAP reads, and Gmail SMTP sends. This is expected for an email skill, but it is a dangerous combination if installed without user trust.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential, IMAP, and SMTP operations are all present in the same script. I found no exfiltration endpoint beyond Gmail services.

content.value_statement: Email triage is slow when messages, replies, and attachments live outside your assistant workflow. This skill lets Claude, Codex, or Claude Code read Gmail and draft threaded replies using 1Password-stored app passwords.
actual_capabilities: Lists recent messages from a selected Gmail folder.
Reads full email content with sender, recipient, subject, date, and message ID.
Fetches Gmail credentials from a named 1Password item.
Replies to existing emails while preserving threading headers.
Sends replies with optional local file attachments.
Supports IMAP search query examples for common mailbox filters.
limitations: Requires the 1Password CLI and an existing Gmail app password.
Supports Gmail IMAP and SMTP, not general email providers.
The reply script sends mail unless dry-run mode is used.
Attachment paths are local files and should be reviewed before sending.

use_cases.title: Review Important Messages
use_cases.description: List and read recent Gmail messages without leaving the assistant session.

use_cases.title: Prepare Threaded Replies
use_cases.description: Reply to an existing email while preserving message threading headers.

use_cases.title: Send Follow-Ups With Files
use_cases.description: Attach local documents to Gmail replies after reviewing the recipient and message body.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Mail
prompt_templates.prompt: List my 10 most recent emails from the Gmail account named "Gmail Work Claude".

prompt_templates.title: Read a Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Read email ID 46 from "Gmail Work Claude" and summarize the sender, subject, date, and main request.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Reply
prompt_templates.prompt: Read email ID 46 from "Gmail Work Claude" and draft a concise reply. Show me the reply before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Send With Attachment
prompt_templates.prompt: Reply to email ID 46 from "Gmail Work Claude" with my approved message and attach the reviewed project PDF.

output_examples.input: List my latest work Gmail messages.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns recent message IDs with sender names, subjects, and dates so you can choose what to read next.

output_examples.input: Read email ID 46 and summarize it.
output_examples.output: The assistant shows the sender, recipients, subject, date, and a concise summary of the message body.

output_examples.input: Reply to the selected email with the approved response.
output_examples.output: The assistant sends a threaded Gmail reply and reports the recipient, subject, and attachment names.

best_practices: Use Gmail app passwords stored in 1Password, not your main Google password.
Run dry-run mode before sending replies that include attachments.
Review recipient, subject, body, and attachment names before allowing a send.
anti_patterns: Do not install this skill for accounts you would not allow the assistant to read.
Do not let untrusted prompts choose attachment paths or send replies.
Do not store broad personal mailbox credentials under vague 1Password item names.

faq.question: Does this skill need my Gmail password?
faq.answer: It expects a Gmail app password stored in 1Password. The documentation says not to use your regular Google password.

faq.question: Can it read private emails?
faq.answer: Yes. It can fetch full Gmail messages from the configured account through IMAP.

faq.question: Can it send email?
faq.answer: Yes. The reply script can send threaded replies through Gmail SMTP.

faq.question: Can I preview a reply before sending?
faq.answer: Yes. The standalone reply script includes a dry-run mode that shows what would be sent.

faq.question: Does it support attachments?
faq.answer: Yes. Replies can include local file attachments when the provided file path exists.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill handles email credentials, reads mailbox content, and sends messages. These are legitimate features but require user trust.
